name: German White Chocolate Cake
recipeIngredient: 4 ounces White chocolate, melted in half cup boiling water and cooled
recipeInstructions:
CAKE
1. Position the racks in the upper and lower thirds of the oven and preheat to 350°F. Lightly butter the bottom and sides of three 8-inch round cake pans. Line the pans with parchment paper, dusting the bottoms and sides with flour; tap out the excess.
2. In a medium bowl, sift together the cake flour and baking soda. Cream the butter and sugar in a large bowl until light and fluffy, about 5 minutes. Beat in one egg yolk at a time, blending well after each addition. Stir in the melted white chocolate mixture and vanilla. At low speed, alternate adding the sifted flour mixture with buttermilk; do not overbeat. Fold in the coconut and pecans.
3. In a large bowl, beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form. Blend one third of the whites into the cake batter to lighten it; gently fold in the remaining egg whites. Spoon batter evenly into prepared pans.
4. Bake until the cakes spring back when touched in the center and a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean, about 35-40 minutes. Transfer cakes to wire racks and cool for 10 minutes. Invert cakes onto wire racks; carefully peel off parchment paper and allow to cool completely.
FOR THE FROSTING:
5. In a heavy medium saucepan over medium heat, combine evaporated milk, sugar, butter, and egg yolks. Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring constantly. Do not let mixture boil rapidly; lower the heat if necessary. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla, pecans, and coconut. Place the saucepan in a bowl of ice and stir constantly until frosting is cool and slightly thickened.
6. Place one cake layer on serving platter. Spread one fourth of frosting evenly over the cake layer, making sure to spread it all the way to the edges. Top with second layer; spread with one fourth of frosting. Add third cake layer; frost top and sides with remaining frosting.
